chunks.text: Next thing we are going to read in HTML is Semantic Markup. We already know that HTML is a markup language and we also read about Semantic Markup. Semantic Markup means we talk about the meaning of content. We will talk about tags that we can understand by reading them.

The most basic and easiest answer is that things become more meaningful. Our code becomes more meaningful. Meaningful means that our layout becomes more structured. How did we get to know about the website? What website should we show it on? What website should we show it on? What does Chrome usually do? It searches for semantic tags. It searches for semantic tags and then it finds out what is the main content on this website. What is the header and footer on this website? SEO friendly means that our website will improve ranking wise if it is semantically good. If we have implemented the semantic markup well, then it becomes easy for our browser to detect what information is written on this website. The third benefit is that it makes our website more readable. With the help of semantic markup, the website becomes more readable. Apart from this, screen readers use screen readers on the website. They use the website to tell different elements on the website. They listen and understand what content is written on a website. Screen readers perform better with the help of semantic markup. We can understand that our user interface improves more. We won't say user interface, but user experience improves more. These are the main three reasons which we should use in any website, project or semantic markup.
